title: Update Regarding Potential Acquisition of APN 019-232-69 (0 Lakeside Drive)
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: March 17, 2026
TO: Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Kerrie Koski, Director of Public Works
Nathan Ullyot, Director Parks & Recreation
DEPT: Public Works
SUBJECT: Update Regarding Potential Acquisition of APN 019-232-69 (0 Lakeside Drive)
Through Washoe County Delinquent Tax Process Pursuant to NRS 361.603
Background
At the February 11, 2026 City Council meeting, the Parks and Recreation Department identified
the 0.51-acre parcel located at 0 Lakeside Drive (APN 019-232-69), adjacent to Virginia Lake
Dog Park, as a potential acquisition (shown on Exhibit 1). The 0.51-acre parcel located at 0 Lakeside Drive (APN 019-232-69) was on the 2025 tax
delinquent list provided by the Washoe County Treasurer's Office, and Public Works and Parks & Recreation staff initiated the process
for potential City of Reno acquisition of the 0.51-acre parcel located at 0 Lakeside Drive (APN 019-232-69) through the delinquent tax process by obtaining City Council support.

If acquired, the 0.51-acre parcel located at 0 Lakeside Drive (APN 019-232-69) could have provided an opportunity to expand the Virginia Lake Dog Park
and help satisfy an outstanding Land and Water Conservation Fund (LWCF) conversion
requirement.

Update
The property owner, Nevada Building & Development LLC, redeemed the 0.51-acre parcel located at 0 Lakeside Drive (APN 019-232-69) by paying the
outstanding delinquent taxes prior to the Washoe County Treasurer's Office's redemption deadline. As a result, the 0.51-acre parcel located at 0 Lakeside Drive (APN 019-232-69)
will not transfer through the delinquent tax process, and the City of Reno is no longer eligible to acquire
the 0.51-acre parcel located at 0 Lakeside Drive (APN 019-232-69) through the delinquent tax process mechanism.

Implications
• The City of Reno's opportunity to acquire the 0.51-acre parcel located at 0 Lakeside Drive (APN 019-232-69) through the delinquent tax process has
expired.
• No City of Reno funds were expended, and no action is required by the City Council.

• Public Works and Parks & Recreation staff will continue evaluating alternative opportunities to address the Land and Water Conservation Fund (LWCF) conversion
requirement, as needed.

Exhibit 1

fields.authors: Kerrie Koski, Director of Public Works; Nathan Ullyot, Director Parks & Recreation
fields.tldr: The City of Reno had been pursuing acquisition of a 0.51-acre parcel at 0 Lakeside Drive (APN 019-232-69), adjacent to Virginia Lake Dog Park, through Washoe County's delinquent tax process. The property owner, Nevada Building & Development LLC, redeemed the parcel by paying outstanding delinquent taxes before the redemption deadline, eliminating the City's acquisition opportunity. No City funds were spent and no Council action is required. Staff will continue exploring alternative ways to satisfy an outstanding Land and Water Conservation Fund conversion requirement.
fields.key_stats: Parcel size: 0.51 acres; APN: 019-232-69; Location: 0 Lakeside Drive, adjacent to Virginia Lake Dog Park; No City funds expended.
fields.key_decisions: No action required by City Council. The acquisition opportunity through the delinquent tax process has expired.
fields.action_items: Public Works and Parks & Recreation staff will continue evaluating alternative opportunities to address the LWCF conversion requirement, as needed.

projects: Land and Water Conservation Fund (LWCF) conversion requirement
dates: February 11, 2026 (City Council meeting where acquisition was identified)
people: Kerrie Koski, Director of Public Works
